     The Numbers Business Report

Real Steel (2011)

Theatrical Performance
Domestic Box Office $85,463,309Details
International Box Office $178,417,032
Worldwide Box Office $263,880,341
Domestic Physical Disc Sales
DVD Sales (estimated) $32,350,001
Blu-ray Sales (estimated) $19,010,912
Total $51,360,913
Further financial details...

Synopsis

Charlie Kenton is a washed-up fighter who lost his chance at a title when 2000-pound, 8-foot-tall steel robots took over the ring. Now nothing but a small-time promoter, Charlie earns just enough money piecing together low-end bots from scrap metal to get from one underground boxing venue to the next. When Charlie hits rock bottom, he reluctantly teams up with his estranged son Max to build and train a championship contender. As the stakes in the brutal, no-holds-barred arena are raised, Charlie and Max, against all odds, get one last shot at a comeback.

Metrics

Opening Weekend:$27,319,677 (32.0% of total gross)
Legs:3.13 (domestic box office/biggest weekend)
Domestic Share:32.4% (domestic box office/worldwide)
Production Budget:$110,000,000 (worldwide box office is 2.4 times production budget)
Theater counts:3,440 opening theaters/3,440 max. theaters, 5.9 weeks average run per theater
Infl. Adj. Dom. BO $128,889,966

Movie Details

Domestic Releases: October 7th, 2011 (Wide) by Walt Disney
October 7th, 2011 (IMAX) by Walt Disney
International Releases: October 21st, 2011 (Wide) (Brazil)
November 25th, 2011 (Wide) (Nigeria)
Video Release: January 24th, 2012 by Walt Disney Home Entertainment
MPA Rating: PG-13 for some violence, intense action and brief language.
(Rating bulletin 2160, 2/23/2011)
Running Time: 126 minutes
Cast, crew, or production detail: Motion Capture Performance
Film format: IMAX: DMR
Plot point: Boxing, Comeback, Dysfunctional Family, Gambling, Robot, Romance, Underdog, Young Child Dealing with the Death of a Parent
Social setting: Organized Crime
Subgenre: Sports Action
Time period setting: Near Future, Yesterday's Tomorrow
Source:Based on Fiction Book/Short Story
Genre:Action
Production Method:Live Action
Creative Type:Science Fiction
Production/Financing Companies: DreamWorks Pictures, Reliance BIG Entertainment, 21 Laps Entertainment, Montford Murphy
Production Countries: United States
Languages: English

Synopsis

Charlie Kenton is a washed-up fighter who lost his chance at a title when 2000-pound, 8-foot-tall steel robots took over the ring. Now nothing but a small-time promoter, Charlie earns just enough money piecing together low-end bots from scrap metal to get from one underground boxing venue to the next. When Charlie hits rock bottom, he reluctantly teams up with his estranged son Max to build and train a championship contender. As the stakes in the brutal, no-holds-barred arena are raised, Charlie and Max, against all odds, get one last shot at a comeback.
